mission.title: Install and Configure Prometheus Operator / kube-prometheus-stack
mission.description: The Prometheus Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Prometheus monitoring stacks on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users looking to automate monitoring configurations and leverage Kubernetes-native resources for observability.

steps.title: Install the Prometheus Operator
steps.description:
Use Helm to install the Prometheus Operator. Make sure to use version v0.77.0 for compatibility with Kubernetes 1.25+.

```bash
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install prometheus prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --version 0.77.0
```

Verify the installation:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n default
kubectl cluster-info
```

steps.title: Configure Prometheus
steps.description:
Create a custom resource definition (CRD) for Prometheus. Save the following YAML to a file named `prometheus.yaml`:

```yaml
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Prometheus
metadata:
  name: prometheus-example
spec:
  serviceAccountName: prometheus-example
  replicas: 1
  podMonitorSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 100m
      memory: 512Mi
```  

Apply the configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f prometheus.yaml
```

Check the status:
```bash
kubectl get prometheus
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Prometheus Operator
uninstall.description:
To uninstall the Prometheus Operator and remove all associated resources, run:
```bash
helm uninstall prometheus
kubectl delete prometheus prometheus-example
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Prometheus Operator
upgrade.description:
To upgrade to a newer version (e.g., v0.77.0 to v0.76.0), first backup your existing Prometheus configuration:
```bash
kubectl get prometheus prometheus-example -o yaml > prometheus-backup.yaml
```

Then, upgrade using Helm:
```bash
helm upgrade prometheus prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --version 0.76.0
```

Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n default
kubectl describe prometheus prometheus-example
```

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus pod not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Prometheus pod is not starting, check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <prometheus-pod-name>
```

Common issues include insufficient resources or misconfigured CRDs. Ensure your cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Alerts not firing
troubleshooting.description:
If alerts are not firing, verify the Alertmanager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get alertmanager
kubectl describe alertmanager <alertmanager-name>
```

Make sure that the alerting rules are correctly defined and that the Alertmanager is reachable.

troubleshooting.title: ServiceMonitor not discovering targets
troubleshooting.description:
If ServiceMonitor is not discovering targets, check the labels on your services:
```bash
kubectl get svc --show-labels
```

Ensure that the ServiceMonitor selector matches the labels of the services you want to monitor.

versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.25.

versionNotes.changes: Added new features for alerting and Grafana dashboards.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed deprecated CRDs from previous versions.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Prometheus Operator running with the Prometheus instance configured and ready to monitor your Kubernetes cluster. You should be able to access the Prometheus UI and see metrics being collected.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install prometheus prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --version 0.77.0

kubectl get pods -n default
kubectl cluster-info

apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Prometheus
metadata:
  name: prometheus-example
spec:
  serviceAccountName: prometheus-example
  replicas: 1
  podMonitorSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 100m
      memory: 512Mi

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (gcloud, aws, az, oci)
prerequisites.description: Ensure that you have a Kubernetes cluster running and that kubectl and Helm are installed and configured.
